architecture_fit.note: Iterated min-relaxation recovers exact distances, but uses hard torch.min (Bellman-Ford), not the soft-min/attention the goal frames, and has no link to base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the built-in one-hop strawman; beats it decisively (lift 0.77 at n=16) but adds no attempt-specific ablation baseline.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic hand-built fn with no model, no hop-ablation, and the README never proposes a causal/faithfulness check.

operating_range.note: Perfect accuracy across the 8->64 sweep, but fixed 10 hops would silently break on larger-diameter graphs and this limit is undiscussed.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-set (no learned params) and reproduces exact Dijkstra distances, though it is essentially the algorithm itself rather than a learned circuit.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is an empty 'Visualisation coming next!' stub; only the shared benchmark panel is shown, so the claim is not made legible interactively.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'Why this visualisation' section largely justifies NOT building a demo view rather than connecting a chart to the goal's question.

notes: The hand-built iterated-min relaxation achieves perfect distance accuracy and robustness (1.0) across the whole node sweep, decisively beating the one-hop baseline. But it implements hard-min Bellman-Ford rather than the soft-min/attention the goal frames, offers no faithfulness/ablation evidence, leaves the fixed 10-hop limit untested at the edges, and ships an empty Demo tab so the result is not visually legible.
